title: mixed_tabs_and_spaces.py example could get auto-reformatted, breaking the illustration
body: mixed_tabs_and_spaces.py intentionally mixes a tab and spaces to demonstrate Python 3's TabError. If a contributor ever runs a repo-wide auto-formatter over this file (or an editor's 'fix whitespace on save' setting is on), the example stops reproducing the error it's meant to show. Could noxfile.py explicitly exclude this file from any lint/format session?

title: String interning examples don't note they're CPython-specific
body: snippets/2_tricky_strings.py asserts things like `a is b` for short string literals, which is true under CPython's interning behavior but not guaranteed by the language spec and can differ under PyPy or future CPython versions. A short note flagging this as an implementation detail (not a language guarantee) would set better expectations.

title: notebook_generator.py isn't documented anywhere
body: irrelevant/notebook_generator.py looks like it builds the Jupyter notebook version of wtfpython from the markdown snippets, but there's no README or CONTRIBUTING mention of when/how to run it after adding a new example. New contributors adding snippets wouldn't know the notebook needs regenerating.

title: obsolete/parse_readme.py looks like dead code left behind
body: irrelevant/obsolete/parse_readme.py sits in an 'obsolete' folder but is still present in the repo tree with no README note explaining why it's kept around or whether it's safe to delete after the build pipeline moved elsewhere. Could it be removed, or at least given a one-line comment explaining its retained purpose?

title: No automated check that README/snippet code blocks still execute
body: noxfile.py defines the project's nox sessions, but none of them appear to actually execute the code snippets shown in the generated book to confirm they still run without error on newer Python versions (several wtfpython examples are version-sensitive). A 'doctest the snippets' session would catch silent breakage.
